sections.title: Rigging Inspection and Pre Lift JHA
sections.html:
RIGGING INSPECTION CHECKLIST

Inspection Frequency: Prior to each shift and as necessary during use per 29 CFR 1926.251(a)(1) and WAC 296-155-33700(1).

Item #

Inspection Item

Pass

Fail

N/A

Corrective Action Required

1

Slings - Synthetic web: No snags, punctures, tears, or cuts (29 CFR 1926.251(e)(8))

☐

☐

☐

2

Slings - Synthetic web: No broken or worn stitches (29 CFR 1926.251(e)(8))

☐

☐

☐

3

Slings - Synthetic web: Identification tags present and legible (29 CFR 1926.251(e)(1))

☐

☐

☐

4

Slings - Wire rope: No excessive broken wires (29 CFR 1926.251(c)(4))

☐

☐

☐

5

Slings - Wire rope: No severe localized abrasion or scraping (29 CFR 1926.251(c)(4))

☐

☐

☐

7

Slings - Wire rope: Identification tags present and legible (29 CFR 1926.251(c)(1))

☐

☐

☐

8

☐

☐

☐

11

Shackles: No deformation, cracks, or excessive wear (29 CFR 1926.251(f)(1))

☐

☐

12

Shackles: Pins are correct type and properly seated (29 CFR 1926.251(f)(1))

☐

☐

☐

13

Hooks: No cracks, nicks, or gouges (29 CFR 1926.251(a)(1))

☐

☐

☐

14

Hooks: Throat opening not increased by more than 15% (29 CFR 1926.251(a)(1))

☐

☐

☐

15

Hooks: Safety latches present and functioning properly (WAC 296-155-33810)

☐

☐

☐

16

Eyebolts/Rings: No cracks, bends, or elongation (29 CFR 1926.251(a)(1))

☐

☐

☐

17

Custom Lifting Devices: Marked with capacity and weight (29 CFR 1926.251(a)(4))

☐

18

Custom Lifting Devices: Structurally sound, no visible defects (29 CFR 1926.251(a)(1))

☐

19

Rigging Hardware: Free of excessive rust or corrosion (29 CFR 1926.251(a)(1))

☐

20

Storage: Rigging stored properly off the ground and protected (WAC 296-155-33700)

Pre-Task Hazard Identification:

☐ Overhead Power Lines

☐ Wind/Weather Conditions

☐ Uneven/Soft Ground

sections.title: Rigging Inspection and Pre Lift JHA (continued 4)
sections.html:
Step #

Task Step Description

Potential Hazard

Hazard Control Measure

Responsible Party

1

Inspect rigging equipment

Using damaged rigging causing load drop

Perform pre-use inspection per checklist. Remove damaged rigging from service.

Rigger

2

Attach rigging to load

Pinch points, sharp edges cutting slings

Keep hands clear of pinch points. Use softeners/edge protection on sharp corners.

Rigger

3

Verify load weight and center of gravity

Overloading crane, load shifting/tipping

Confirm weight with documentation. Perform test lift to verify balance.

Lift Director / Rigger

4

Lift and move load

Struck by load, swing radius hazards

Use taglines to control load. Keep personnel out of fall zone and swing radius.

Operator / Rigger

5

Communicate during lift

Misunderstanding signals causing accident

Establish clear communication method (radio or standard hand signals). Designate one signal person.

Signal Person / Operator

6

7

8

9

10
